title: Bridge Essay: Second Language Word Learning through Repetition and Imitation: Functional Netwo

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Language/Mantra** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Phase Language/Mantra bridge-v3 targeted acquisition.

**Second Language Word Learning through Repetition and Imitation: Functional Networks as a Function of Learning Phase and Language Distance.** — Frontiers in human neuroscience (2017). PMID: 29033804. DOI: 10.3389/fnhum.2017.00463. Score: 40. Introduction and Aim: Repetition and imitation are among the oldest second language (L2) teaching approaches and are frequently used in the context of L2 learning and language therapy, despite some heavy criticism. Current neuroimaging techniques allow the neural mechanisms underlying repetition and imitation to be examined. This fMRI study examines the influence of verbal repetition and imitation on network configuration. Integration changes within and between the cognitive control and language
